introduction: A malignant epithelial neoplasm of the breast. It is the most commonly diagnosed cancer in women and is the leading cause of new cases of female cancers in Nigerian women.

features: Breast: breast lump, bloody nipple discharge, breast or nipple ulceration
Axilla: armpit swelling, upper limb lymphoedema
Metastatic: respiratory (cough, breathlessness), liver (abdominal swelling, jaundice), bones (bone pain, pathological fracture), brain (seizures), spine (paraparesis or paraplegia), etc.

investigations: FNAC/core needle biopsy/incisional or excision biopsy: For histopathological and immunohistochemical analyses
Chest x-ray, abdominal USS, bone scan, with or withoutCT chest/abdomen: For Staging
Others: FBC, LFT, Electrolyte, Urea and Creatinine

drug: Chemotherapy - Neoadjuvant (locally advanced, locoregionally advanced, metastatic)
Combination chemotherapy (depending on immunohistochemistry, performance index, etc.). Examples: EC, AC, FEC, Docetaxel, Capecitabine, etc.
Radiotherapy - Hormonal therapy: dictated by immunohistochemistry
Tamoxifen: for premenopausal women, 20 mg daily for at least 5 years; for postmenopausal women with shifting to aromatase inhibitors later
supportive_measures: Lifestyle modifications (reduce exposure to estrogen, quit alcohol and smoking, etc)
Periodic imaging e.g. mammogram, chest x-ray, abdominal USS, bone scan

definitive_treatment: Mastectomy (Simple mastectomy with axillary clearance, modified radical mastectomy)
